description_long: A shallow, round terracotta-colored bowl holds several garlic bulbs, each with a pale, off-white to light tan papery skin. The bulbs are plump and irregularly shaped, with some showing the fibrous root tuft at their base and others partially covered by their outer skin. The cloves within each bulb are tightly packed, creating a bumpy, segmented surface. The bowl itself has a smooth, slightly glossy finish and a warm, earthy hue. The garlic bulbs display natural variations in color, with some areas appearing more yellowish or brownish. The overall appearance suggests a fresh, rustic presentation suitable for a kitchen or market setting.
description: A terracotta-colored bowl filled with several plump garlic bulbs, some with visible root tufts and papery skins, displaying natural color variations.
description_view_0: Three garlic bulbs are clearly visible, with one showing a prominent root tuft and papery skin texture.
description_view_1: The view highlights the rounded backs of the garlic bulbs, with less detail on the root tufts and more focus on the smooth, curved surfaces.
description_view_2: A close look at the root tuft of one bulb and the side of another, with the bowl's rim framing the arrangement.
description_view_3: Multiple bulbs are visible with two root tufts exposed, and the papery skin texture is more pronounced.
attribute_bulb_segmentation: The garlic bulbs are whole with visible segmentation into individual cloves; 3-4 segments are discernible per bulb, with moderate clarity.
attribute_clove_arrangement: The cloves are tightly packed within each bulb, forming a compact, rounded cluster with a natural, slightly irregular pattern.
attribute_root_tuft_visibility: visible, with fibrous, light brown tufts at the base of at least two bulbs
